steps.title: Set equations equal and solve for x
steps.content: At the intersection point, both equations yield the same $y$-value. Set them equal: $$(x-2)^2 = 4x - 12$$ Expand the left side: $$x^2 - 4x + 4 = 4x - 12$$ Rearrange: $$x^2 - 8x + 16 = 0$$ This is a perfect square trinomial: $$(x-4)^2 = 0$$ Therefore, $x = 4$.

steps.title: Substitute to find the y-coordinate
steps.content: Using $y = 4x - 12$: $$y = 4(4) - 12 = 16 - 12 = 4$$ The $y$-coordinate of the intersection point is 4.
